features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox have focused on enhancing its media handling capabilities and simplifying the code behind it. A key addition is the implementation of the **Picture In Picture API**, which allows users to watch videos in a small window that stays on top of other applications, making multitasking easier. Additionally, internal improvements to how the browser manages audio streams and strengthens references ensure better performance and reliability when decoding media. These changes not only streamline the browser's architecture but also contribute to a more robust user experience for consuming various media formats.

features.summary: Recent changes to the Firefox browser have focused on improving how audio sessions are managed during web browsing. This includes the addition of a dedicated system for tracking audio session states, which helps ensure that audio from different tabs behaves correctly when users interact with the browser. In particular, developers implemented algorithms that facilitate smooth transitions between different audio states, making it easier to manage background audio and improve user experience. These enhancements ultimately support better audio handling, particularly for applications that rely on features like _Web Audio_ and _Media Source Extensions_ for streaming content. As a result, users can expect **more reliable and consistent audio behavior across various websites**, especially when switching between tabs or using the picture-in-picture mode to watch videos.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Media Capabilities within Firefox have focused on enhancing support for **WebRTC, the technology behind video calls**, ensuring it operates more smoothly and efficiently. This includes creating a mechanism to validate WebRTC’s scalability settings and implementing allowlists for various MediaCapabilities types to streamline support checks. Additional improvements have also been made to how the browser handles audio and video codecs, enhancing its ability to decode and encode content while optimizing power efficiency. These changes are critical as they not only improve the performance of real-time communications but also ensure broader compatibility with various media formats and configurations, making for a better user experience during streaming and video calls.

features.summary: Recent updates to the media control features in Firefox have significantly improved how the browser manages audio sessions, enhancing the user experience when multiple audio sources are active. The updates introduce a new way to track and manage audio session states, allowing for **more effective handling of audio focus, which means the right audio source plays without confusion when several apps or tabs are open**. Implementations of various algorithms ensure that audio types are correctly identified and managed in real-time, while tests have been added to confirm stability even when audible iframes (like embedded media) appear. This means users can enjoy a smoother audio experience while navigating the web, with less interference from other sources.
